prompts: Benzene, C6H6, has two primary resonance structures. Each structure consists of a hexagonal ring of carbon atoms, with alternating single and double bonds between adjacent carbons. The first structure has double bonds between carbons 1-2, 3-4, and 5-6, while the second has double bonds between carbons 2-3, 4-5, and 6-1. In both structures, each carbon atom is also bonded to a single hydrogen atom. Resonance in benzene indicates electron delocalization within the pi bonds across the ring, contributing to its stability.
